title: P1.2 — Dependency analyzer (regex import extraction)
description:
Implement `internal/deps/`: regex-based import extraction for TS/JS, Go, Python (`analyzer.go`). Resolve relative paths to repo-relative file IDs. Produce Road edges with confidence levels (`graph.go`). These are best-effort, not ground truth.
Priority: 4
Labels: backend, deps
Depends: P1.1 — Core data model types
---

title: Mouse drag to pan the city
description: Add click-and-drag panning on the canvas. Currently only keyboard (WASD/arrows) and scroll-wheel zoom are supported. Should feel natural — mousedown starts, mousemove pans camera, mouseup stops. Must not conflict with click-to-select.
close_reason: merged to main at 153bb2a — mouse drag to pan

title: P2: Dispatch wizard UI (DispatchWizard, ScopeSelector, CommandPalette)
description: Implement web/src/orchestration/ — the Phase 2 dispatch UI. Three-step wizard triggered by D key or Cmd+K: (1) select scope via lasso/click, (2) choose role (fix-bug, refactor, review, add-test, docs, optimize, custom), (3) dispatch to agent. Includes DispatchWizard.tsx, ScopeSelector.tsx, CommandPalette.tsx. See DESIGN.md P2.1 and sketch-E for full spec. Phase 2 must be explicit opt-in — default app stays passive.

title: Go: GET /api/buildings/{id} can't match IDs with slashes
description: Building IDs are paths like internal/hub/hub.go. The {id} wildcard captures one segment only. Use {id...} rest wildcard so PathValue returns the full path.
close_reason: merged to main at cbdacd2 — {id...} rest wildcard for slashed building IDs

title: Docs: Fix incorrect make dev command in DESIGN.md
description: DESIGN.md line 734 shows 'make dev REPO=/path/to/target/repo' as the backend development command. This is wrong in two ways: (1) the Makefile dev target runs 'cd web && npm run dev' which is the frontend, not the backend; (2) the Makefile dev target doesn't accept a REPO variable. The correct backend command is 'go run ./cmd/agent-city --repo=/path/to/repo'. CLAUDE.md has the correct commands. Fix: update DESIGN.md's Build & Run section to match reality.

title: P1.4 — Session persistence (localStorage)
description:
Persist camera position, zoom level, selected building, panel states, and toggle states to localStorage keyed by repo path. Restore viewport on reload.
Priority: 7
Labels: frontend
Depends: P1.1 — React + Canvas scaffold with isometric projection
---

title: Docs: README needs vision statement and pointer to DESIGN.md
description: The README is extremely thin -- 34 lines with a one-line description ('Manage AI coding agents like a SimCity mayor'), quick start, keyboard shortcuts, and stack. It does not explain: (1) what the project actually does (live visualization of codebase as isometric city with AI agent activity), (2) the two-phase vision (Phase 1: see the city, Phase 2: run the city), (3) current status (what works now), or (4) where to find more detail (no link to DESIGN.md). A fresh agent or human landing on this repo gets a tagline and build commands but no understanding of scope, goals, or architecture. Fix: add 2-3 sentences explaining the core concept (codebase-as-city visualization, real-time agent tracking), a 'Learn more' section linking to DESIGN.md, and a one-line current status note.

title: Docs: Deduplicate CLAUDE.md package layout and build instructions against DESIGN.md
description: CLAUDE.md's 'Quick orientation' package layout and 'Build & run' section substantially overlap with DESIGN.md's 'Package Layout' and 'Build & Run' sections. CLAUDE.md has a simplified version; DESIGN.md has the full version. Since both files are loaded every session (CLAUDE.md always, DESIGN.md when referenced), they will drift as the codebase evolves -- and they've already diverged (CLAUDE.md is missing internal/city, DESIGN.md has a wrong make dev command). Fix: keep the authoritative package layout and build instructions in DESIGN.md. In CLAUDE.md, trim the package layout to just the top-level structure (cmd/, internal/, web/src/) and link to DESIGN.md for the full breakdown. Keep build commands in CLAUDE.md since agents need them every session, but ensure they match DESIGN.md exactly.

title: Pressing R for roads toggle does nothing
description: The R key is supposed to toggle road visibility but pressing it has no visible effect. Either the keybinding isn't wired up, the road rendering isn't working, or there's no visual feedback that the state changed.

title: Go: JSON diff is positional-only for arrays — defeats incremental patching
description: Diff function does positional array comparison. Inserting one building at index 0 generates N replace ops. primitiveEqual marshals both values to JSON strings for every leaf comparison. Zero test coverage on the most critical protocol piece. Consider keyed diffing by ID for arrays of objects, and add thorough tests.
close_reason: merged to main at d34a44e — keyed array diffing + type-safe primitiveEqual

title: P1.1 — Go backend skeleton with demo mode
description:
Set up the Go backend entry point (`cmd/agent-city/main.go`) serving embedded static files. Implement demo mode (`--demo` flag) generating synthetic city data: 5 districts, ~30 buildings with randomized LOC/coverage/language/status, 8 synthetic agents. Wire up the HTTP server with stdlib `net/http` routing.
Priority: 1
Labels: backend, skeleton
---

title: P1.2 — WebSocket hub (full state + patches)
description:
Implement `internal/hub/`: WebSocket server with client registry and broadcast (`hub.go`), CityState assembly and JSON patch diff computation (`state.go`). Full snapshot on connect, incremental patches on change. Two cadences: agent state ~100ms tick, repo structure on-change (debounced).
Priority: 3
Labels: backend, websocket
Depends: P1.1 — REST API endpoints
---

title: Go: no tests for hub, layout, or api packages
description: Only internal/deps and internal/repo have tests. Hub (concurrency + protocol), layout (treemap math), and API handlers have zero coverage. Minimum: Diff correctness, client lifecycle, treemap no-overlap invariant, handler 200/404 responses.
notes: merge conflict: 3 files (hub_test.go, state_test.go, handlers_test.go) have structural conflicts with recent merges. Branch preserved for manual resolution.

title: Wire frontend to live backend state instead of hard-coded demo city
description: App.tsx always calls setCity(makeDemoCity()) and never calls startWs(), so the UI does not consume /ws or /api/state. This makes the implemented WebSocket hub, watcher, scanner, and live backend mostly invisible in the product.
acceptance_criteria: Live mode connects to /ws on mount, applies state.full and state.patch, cleans up on unmount, and no longer overwrites live state with demo data. Demo mode remains explicit and does not leak into normal runs.

title: Pressing M for minimap toggle does nothing
description: The M key is supposed to toggle the minimap but pressing it has no visible effect. Either the keybinding isn't wired up or the minimap isn't implemented/rendering.

title: P1.1 — Core data model types
description:
Define all core Go types in `internal/model/model.go`: CityState, RepoInfo, District, Building, Road, Agent, ActivityEvent, RepoStats. These are the shared types used across all backend packages.
Priority: 1
Labels: backend, model
---

title: UI polish: cursor styling, hover effects, responsive edges
description: Visual polish pass: pointer cursor on clickable elements (buildings, agents), hover highlight on mouseover, ensure HUD panels don't overflow on narrow viewports. Low priority — functional UX first.
close_reason: merged to main at c63274c — cursor styling, hover effects, responsive HUD

title: P1.1 — React + Canvas scaffold with isometric projection
description:
Set up the frontend: React 18 + TypeScript + Vite. Implement the canvas render pipeline with isometric projection (ported from sketch-A-v2.jsx). Grid background, building rendering as wireframe boxes, pan/zoom camera. Zustand store for city state.
Priority: 1
Labels: frontend, canvas
Depends: P1.1 — Core data model types
---

title: P1.3 — Agent roster and activity log in HUD
description:
Wire agent data into the LeftRail (roster with progress bars, model tier, activity state) and RightRail (activity log with interleaved color-coded events). Keyboard: 1-9 jump to agent, G/Shift+G cycle agents, [ ] focus panels, I inspect agent.
Priority: 5
Labels: frontend, hud, agents
Depends: P1.1 — HUD: TopBar, LeftRail, RightRail, BottomStrip, P1.3 — UFO renderer (agents, tractor beams, flight paths)
---

title: P1.4 — Scan line overlay, vignette, and grid polish
description:
Add visual polish layers: scan line overlay, vignette effect, refined grid background (12px + 60px lines matching sd-paper). These are the final render pipeline layers from the prototype.
Priority: 7
Labels: frontend, canvas
Depends: P1.1 — React + Canvas scaffold with isometric projection
---

title: Go backend: repo scanner + WebSocket push
description: Scan the local repo (files, directories, dependencies) and map them to CityState (buildings, districts, roads). Push state.full snapshots and state.patch incremental updates over the /ws WebSocket endpoint. The frontend wsMiddleware.ts is already wired to receive these messages.
close_reason: merged to main at 3db773c — repo scanner + WebSocket push

title: Implement useCameraControls hook
description: Create web/src/hooks/useCameraControls.ts — a hook that encapsulates pan/zoom from both keyboard and pointer input. Currently camera control logic may be spread across useCityKeyboard and canvas code. This hook should be the single owner of camera state mutations (pan, zoom, reset) and expose them cleanly. See DESIGN.md hooks section.

title: P1.4 — Keyboard shortcut overlay and high-contrast mode
description:
Implement `?` key overlay showing all keyboard shortcuts organized by category. High-contrast mode toggle (`C` key) boosting all foreground opacity to 1.0.
Priority: 6
Labels: frontend, accessibility
Depends: P1.1 — Keyboard navigation (H/J/K/L cursor, Tab, Enter)
---

title: Extract theme module (colors.ts, typography.ts)
description: Create web/src/theme/ with colors.ts and typography.ts. colors.ts should extract the solarized dark palette currently in web/src/hud/palette.ts into a shared theme module. typography.ts should define font sizes matching the design prototypes in code-sim/project/sketches/sd-helpers.jsx. Both canvas renderers and HUD components should import from theme/ instead of duplicating values.

title: P1.1 — District renderer (diamond outlines, labels)
description:
Implement `DistrictRenderer.ts`: dashed diamond outlines for districts, district labels. Draw back-to-front by gx+gy.
Priority: 1
Labels: frontend, canvas
Depends: P1.1 — React + Canvas scaffold with isometric projection
---

title: Docs: Mark not-yet-implemented files in CLAUDE.md and DESIGN.md package layouts
description: Both CLAUDE.md and DESIGN.md list files and directories in their package layout trees that don't exist yet: web/src/orchestration/ (Phase 2), web/src/theme/ (colors.ts, typography.ts), web/src/hooks/useCameraControls.ts, web/src/hooks/useWebSocket.ts, internal/agents/ (all files). These are aspirational -- describing the target architecture -- but a fresh agent takes package layout sections as ground truth about what's on disk. Fix: either (1) annotate planned-but-not-implemented entries with a marker like '— (planned, Phase 2)' or '— (not yet implemented)', or (2) split the layout into 'Current' and 'Planned' sections. This is especially important for CLAUDE.md which is loaded into every session's context.

title: Go: activities list grows unboundedly
description: model.CityState.Activities has no cap. In live mode with active agents, it grows forever, bloating every state.full snapshot and diff. Cap at ~100-200 entries, evict oldest on append.
close_reason: merged to main at 6df8a9e — cap activities at 200, evict oldest

title: Go: hub broadcast channel can deadlock — sender and reader are same goroutine
description: maybeBroadcastPatch sends on h.broadcast from inside Run(). If the 256-deep buffer fills (slow clients), the hub goroutine blocks itself forever. Fix: fan out directly to clients from maybeBroadcastPatch instead of routing through the channel.
close_reason: merged to main at e10ecd5 — fan-out broadcast, drop slow clients

title: Makefile still uses old binary name agent-city instead of agentic-city
description: The Makefile run target uses 'go run ./cmd/agent-city' but the binary/project has been renamed to agentic-city. Update the Makefile and any other references to use the new name.

title: Go: missing modelTier field in Agent struct
description: Frontend expects Agent.modelTier (opus/sonnet/haiku/unknown) but Go model.Agent has no corresponding field. Add ModelTier string json:modelTier,omitempty to model.Agent.
close_reason: merged to main at aa02ccb — added ModelTier field to Agent struct

title: [ ] focus left/right feature is confusing
description: The [ and ] keys for focus left/right are not intuitive. It's unclear what they do or what 'focus' means in this context. Needs better UX — either make the behavior more obvious with visual feedback, rename/rethink the concept, or remove if not useful.

title: P1.2 — Road renderer (dependency edges)
description:
Implement `RoadRenderer.ts`: dashed lines on ground plane for dependency edges. Toggleable via `R` key. Faint by default, highlighted when relevant to selection. Confidence-scored rendering (exact vs inferred vs weak).
Priority: 4
Labels: frontend, canvas
Depends: P1.2 — Dependency analyzer (regex import extraction), P1.1 — React + Canvas scaffold with isometric projection
---

title: Go: graceful shutdown — signal handling + context cancellation
description: main() calls http.ListenAndServe which blocks forever. No signal handling (SIGINT/SIGTERM), no graceful shutdown, no hub context cancellation. WebSocket connections get no CloseMessage on kill. Fix: signal.NotifyContext, http.Server.Shutdown, wire cancellation through hub.Run.
close_reason: merged to main at bf86b56 — graceful shutdown with signal handling + context cancellation

title: Go: watcher silently swallows all fsnotify errors
description: fw.Errors channel is read and discarded with no logging. If inotify hits its watch limit, the watcher silently stops tracking new directories. Log at warn level, add specific message for ENOSPC suggesting sysctl fix.
close_reason: merged to main at a74e1d6 — log fsnotify errors + ENOSPC hint

title: Test coverage windows should be transparent with colored border, not filled
description: The test coverage indicators rendered as 'windows' on buildings are currently filled-in colored squares. They should instead be transparent with just a single colored border outline. This will look cleaner and not obscure the building face.

title: P1.1 — Treemap layout engine
description:
Implement `internal/layout/`: squarified treemap for district placement (`treemap.go`), shelf-pack buildings within districts (`packer.go`), and the top-level engine (`engine.go`) that converts files into positioned districts + buildings. Deterministic sort by path, configurable `--district-depth` (default: 2).
Priority: 1
Labels: backend, layout
Depends: P1.1 — Core data model types
---

title: Go: run go mod tidy — direct deps marked indirect
description: gorilla/websocket and fsnotify are directly imported but marked // indirect in go.mod. Run go mod tidy to fix markers.
close_reason: merged to main at 15a77b5 — go mod tidy

title: P1.1 — Vite config with proxy to Go backend
description:
Set up `vite.config.ts`: dev server on :5173 proxying `/api/*` and `/ws` to Go backend on :8080. TypeScript config, HMR.
Priority: 1
Labels: frontend, build

title: P2: Alarm overlay and rapid-response dispatch
description: Implement AlarmOverlay.tsx and RapidResponse.tsx in web/src/orchestration/. When test failures or build breaks are detected: red pulsing vignette, CRITICAL banner with blast radius, lightning paths from error origin to affected dependencies, affected buildings flash red with smoke. Includes one-click 'send agent to fix' from alarm panel. See DESIGN.md P2.3 and sketch-D for full spec.

title: Go: CityState deep-copied + serialized every 100ms tick unconditionally
description: State.GetState() copies by value (slices share backing arrays — data race risk). Then maybeBroadcastPatch marshals+unmarshals+diffs every 100ms regardless of changes. Fix: add dirty flag to skip no-op ticks, and either deep-copy slices or return pre-marshaled bytes from GetState.
close_reason: merged to main — dirty flag + deep copy for CityState ticks

title: P1.3 — Agentwatch monitor setup
description: Implement `internal/agents/monitor.go`: configure agentwatch sources (Claude, Codex, Gemini), create a `monitor.Monitor`, implement a `monitor.EventSink` bridge that feeds session state changes into the city state. Run the monitor loop in a goroutine. Graceful degradation when no agent sessions are detected (show city without agents). Uses `go get github.com/mrf/agentwatch` — no external server required.

title: README: pithy with screenshot, not a novel
description: Write a short README.md. One-liner description, screenshot, quick start (3-4 commands max), keyboard shortcuts table, MIT license badge. No walls of text, no 'Contributing' section longer than the code, no badges nobody reads. Let the screenshot do the talking.
close_reason: merged to main at 6ff2fdc — pithy README

title: Remove or redesign corner shape indicators (diamond/triangle/square)
description: There are red diamonds, yellow triangles, and grey squares rendered in the corners of buildings. Their meaning is unclear and they don't look good. Either remove them entirely or redesign with something more intuitive.

title: Docs: Add or remove screenshot reference in README
description: README.md line 8 references docs/screenshot.png which does not exist. This is a broken image link on the repo's front door. Either: (1) capture a screenshot from demo mode and add it at docs/screenshot.png, or (2) remove the image reference until one exists. A broken image link on the landing page signals neglect to both humans and AI agents assessing the project.

title: Docs: Document internal/city package in CLAUDE.md and DESIGN.md
description: The internal/city package (builder.go, builder_test.go) is a real, active package imported by main.go that wires together repo scanning, layout engine, and dependency analysis via BuildState() and MergeBuildings(). Neither CLAUDE.md's 'Quick orientation' nor DESIGN.md's 'Package Layout' mention it. An agent looking at the directory structure will find an undocumented package, and an agent reading only the docs will miss this critical wiring layer. Fix: add internal/city/ to both package layout sections with a brief description like 'city builder — wires scan + layout + deps into CityState'.

title: Write CLAUDE.md with strict TDD and A+ coding standards
description: Create a project CLAUDE.md that establishes coding standards for the codebase. Go: strict TDD (test first), aim for A+ on Go Report Card (golint, govet, gofmt, gocyclo, ineffassign, misspell), enforce error handling, no naked returns, structured logging. TypeScript: strict mode, no any, ESLint clean, test coverage for all new code. Both: test-first workflow — failing test before implementation. Document project structure, build commands, and test commands so any agent dropping in cold can orient fast.
close_reason: merged to main at 0adccd7 — CLAUDE.md with TDD and A+ coding standards

title: Keyboard +/- zoom increments too small
description: Zooming with keyboard + and - keys takes too many presses to get anywhere. The zoom step size is too small, making it tedious to zoom in/out. Need to increase the zoom increment so fewer keypresses are needed to traverse the zoom range.

title: Docs: Add implementation status to DESIGN.md or README
description: DESIGN.md describes the full vision (Phase 1 + Phase 2, all packages, all hooks, orchestration UI) but never says what's actually built vs. what's planned. A fresh agent session reads the DESIGN.md and assumes internal/agents/, web/src/orchestration/, web/src/theme/, and hooks like useCameraControls.ts and useWebSocket.ts all exist -- they don't. The briefing in docs/briefings/2026-05-08.md partially tracks this but it's dated and not linked from CLAUDE.md or README. Need: a 'Current State' section (probably in DESIGN.md near the top, after 'Vision: Two Phases') that clearly marks which P1 milestones are complete, which packages exist, and which are blocked/not-yet-started. This prevents agents from trying to import packages that don't exist yet or building on top of nonexistent infrastructure.

title: Move angled labels to bottom-left of buildings
description: The angled labels on building components look good but are positioned on the bottom-right side. They should be on the bottom-left side instead.

title: P1.4 — Minimap
description:
Implement minimap overlay showing the full city at a reduced scale with agent positions. Toggleable via `M` key. Shows viewport rectangle for orientation.
Priority: 6
Labels: frontend, hud
Depends: P1.1 — React + Canvas scaffold with isometric projection
---

title: P1.1 — Repo scanner (Git tree walk)
description:
Implement `internal/repo/scanner.go`: walk Git tree via go-git, compute per-file LOC, detect language by extension. Apply file filtering (gitignore, known non-source dirs, binary detection). Configurable `--exclude` globs and `--max-depth`.
Priority: 1
Labels: backend, repo
Depends: P1.1 — Core data model types
---

title: Go: WebSocket upgrader allows all origins
description: CheckOrigin returns true unconditionally — any page can open a WS and read full CityState. Also api.Server has a dead upgrader field never used when hub handler is wired. Fix: check Origin matches Host, remove dead upgrader.
close_reason: merged to main at 662415e — origin check + dead upgrader removed

title: Auto-fit initial zoom to fill viewport with city
description: On load, calculate the bounding box of all buildings/districts and set the camera zoom and offset so the city fills most of the available window. Currently starts at scale=1.0 with a fixed offset, which can leave the city too small or off-center depending on viewport size and city extents.
close_reason: merged to main at 0bcea76 — auto-fit zoom on load

title: Move UFOs further from city to reduce visual clutter
description: Agent UFOs are currently too close to the city buildings, making it hard to see the full city layout. Move UFOs further out and away from the city perimeter so the city itself is always clearly visible. Consider angling/spreading the UFO positions to further reduce visual overlap. The city should be easy to read at a glance even with multiple agents active.

title: P1.2 — Zustand store with WebSocket middleware
description:
Implement `web/src/store/`: cityStore (CityState from backend), uiStore (selection, zoom, camera state), wsMiddleware (WebSocket connect/reconnect/dispatch). Handle full state snapshots and incremental patches.
Priority: 3
Labels: frontend, state
Depends: P1.1 — React + Canvas scaffold with isometric projection
---

title: P1.1 — HUD: TopBar, LeftRail, RightRail, BottomStrip
description:
Implement the four HUD overlay components as React HTML/CSS positioned over the canvas. TopBar: repo/branch/SHA/CI/tests. LeftRail: agent roster + progress bars. RightRail: selected building details, activity log, stats. BottomStrip: keyboard shortcuts, status ticker. Use solarized dark palette and JetBrains Mono font.
Priority: 2
Labels: frontend, hud
Depends: P1.1 — React + Canvas scaffold with isometric projection
---
close_reason: merged to main at b8b5fb6 (conflict resolved: App.tsx onClick+HudOverlay)

title: P1.1 — Keyboard navigation (H/J/K/L cursor, Tab, Enter)
description:
Implement `useCityKeyboard` hook: spatial cursor with amber highlight ring, H/J/K/L directional movement mapped to isometric axes, Tab/Shift+Tab cycling within district, Enter to select, Escape to deselect. Camera auto-follows cursor. Bottom strip shows contextual shortcuts. This is a Phase 1 requirement, not polish.
Priority: 1
Labels: frontend, keyboard, accessibility
Depends: P1.1 — React + Canvas scaffold with isometric projection
---

title: Implement useWebSocket hook
description: Create web/src/hooks/useWebSocket.ts — a hook encapsulating WebSocket connection lifecycle (connect, reconnect, disconnect, status). Currently WS logic lives in web/src/store/wsMiddleware.ts as Zustand middleware. This hook should provide a clean React-side API for connection status and manual reconnect, wrapping the existing middleware. See DESIGN.md hooks section.

title: P1.2 — Metrics sources (coverage, test results)
description:
Implement `internal/repo/metrics.go`: parse coverage output files (Go coverage.out, lcov, coverage.py). Parse test result files (JUnit XML, Go test JSON). Map test failures to specific files for building status. Unknown data defaults to unknown status, never to ok. Re-parse when coverage files change via fsnotify.
Priority: 4
Labels: backend, metrics
Depends: P1.2 — File watcher with debounced incremental updates

title: P1.1 — REST API endpoints
description:
Implement `internal/api/`: HTTP server with route registration (`server.go`), REST handlers (`handlers.go`): GET /api/state (full CityState snapshot), GET /api/buildings/{id} (single building detail). WebSocket upgrade endpoint at /ws.
Priority: 1
Labels: backend, api
Depends: P1.1 — Core data model types

title: P1.4 — Building visual details (window dots, status pips, edit rings)
description:
Add building visual details: window dots when coverage is known (coverage × pseudo-random pattern), status pips with distinct shapes (ok=circle, warn=triangle, err=diamond+blink, unknown=hollow square). Yellow edit-pulse rings on roof when editing=true. Color-blind safe: shape+color for all status indicators.
Priority: 6
Labels: frontend, canvas, accessibility
Depends: P1.1 — Building renderer (wireframe boxes, labels)

title: P1.2 — File watcher with debounced incremental updates
description:
Implement `internal/repo/watcher.go`: fsnotify on repo root, 500ms debounce, re-scan only changed files. Layout recalculates only when files are added/removed/moved (not content-only edits).
Priority: 3
Labels: backend, repo
Depends: P1.1 — Repo scanner (Git tree walk)

title: Increase max zoom level for close-up inspection
description: Current MAX_SCALE is 10.0 in IsometricCamera.ts. Users still want to zoom in further to inspect individual buildings and agent details up close. Raise the cap and verify rendering holds up at high zoom (line weights, label sizes, window detail).
close_reason: merged to main at f606f67 — increased max zoom

title: P1.1 — Building renderer (wireframe boxes, labels)
description:
Implement `BuildingRenderer.ts`: isometric boxes with footprint, hidden back edges, base outline, side faces (language-tinted), roof. Floating labels with backing plates. Back-to-front draw order by gx+gy for correct occlusion.
Priority: 1
Labels: frontend, canvas
Depends: P1.1 — React + Canvas scaffold with isometric projection
---
notes: orchestrator: session idle at prompt with 0 commits — possible failure. Will retry if still idle next turn.

title: P2: Agent spawning backend (worktree + tmux + CLI launch)
description: Implement the backend for dispatching agents from the UI. After dispatch confirmation, agent-city should: spawn a Claude Code session in a new worktree + tmux window, target selected files with chosen role as prompt, agentwatch detects the new session on next poll. Shell out to git worktree add + tmux new-window + claude --worktree. Must validate repo is clean, branch name is unique, CLI is installed. Failures leave clear activity event and no partial UI state. See DESIGN.md P2.2.

title: District labels: angled text along bottom edge of dashed box
description: District labels currently render as flat text. Reposition them to follow along the bottom edge of the district's dashed outline box, angled to match the isometric edge. This makes them easier to read and visually tied to their district boundary rather than floating.
close_reason: merged to main at 97aa4e1 — angled district labels along bottom edge

title: P1.3 — Agent tracker (session-to-city mapping)
description: Implement `internal/agents/tracker.go`: map agentwatch `session.SessionState` objects to city agents. Parse model tier (opus/sonnet/haiku) from `SessionState.Model` string. Map `SessionState.Activity` to mode (work/fly/idle/error). Confidence-scored agent-to-building mapping using fsnotify file events and `SessionState.WorkingDir`. Consumes sessions from the agentwatch `EventSink` bridge (ac-c9u) and file watcher events.

title: Go: watcher debounce can starve delivery indefinitely
description: Every fsnotify event resets the 500ms debounce timer. Continuous file writes (e.g. formatter) prevent the batch from ever flushing. Fix: fixed-window debounce with a max duration (e.g. 2s) that flushes regardless of new events.
close_reason: merged to main at 5a2a21f — fixed-window debounce with 2s max

title: P1.3 — UFO renderer (agents, tractor beams, flight paths)
description:
Implement `AgentRenderer.ts`: UFO rendering with dome + disc + portholes. Color by model family (blue=Claude, green=Codex, orange=Gemini). Size by tier (haiku=0.6x, sonnet=1.0x, opus=1.4x). Dome glow for thinking state. Tractor beams (trapezoid + scan lines + impact ellipse) with solid/dotted rendering per location confidence. Flying UFOs on parametric bezier curves. `AnimationManager.ts` for tween flight, pulse, blink.
Priority: 5
Labels: frontend, canvas, agents
Depends: P1.1 — React + Canvas scaffold with isometric projection
---

title: P1.4 — Hit testing for isometric shapes
description:
Implement `HitTester.ts`: click/cursor detection on isometric building shapes. Convert screen coordinates to grid coordinates accounting for camera transform. Support building selection by click and cursor proximity for keyboard navigation.
Priority: 2
Labels: frontend, canvas
Depends: P1.1 — React + Canvas scaffold with isometric projection
---

title: Wire up MetricsWatcher so live mode shows real coverage and test status
description:
## Problem

In live mode, every building gets `Coverage: -1` (unknown) at scan time (`internal/repo/scanner.go:379`), and the `MetricsWatcher` is never started. The coverage feature shows zero for all files even when coverage data exists in the repo.

Demo mode works fine because `makeDemoBuildings` generates random coverage values directly.

## Root cause

`MetricsWatcher` (`internal/repo/metrics.go`) is fully implemented — parsers for Go coverage.out, LCOV, coverage.py JSON, JUnit XML, and Go test JSON all exist and are tested — but nothing in `main.go` creates or starts a `MetricsWatcher`. There is no path from the parsed metrics data to the building state.

Specifically:
- `BuildConfig` has no `MetricsConfig` field
- `main.go` has no CLI flags for coverage/test-result file paths
- No goroutine consumes `MetricsWatcher.Updates` to apply coverage/status to buildings

## Fix

1. Auto-detect common coverage/test-result files in the repo root (e.g. `coverage.out`, `lcov.info`, `coverage.json`, `test-results.xml`, `test-results.json`)
2. Add CLI flags (`--coverage`, `--test-results`) for explicit path overrides
3. Start a `MetricsWatcher` in `main.go` alongside the existing `runWatcher`
4. When metrics updates arrive, apply `MetricsSource.Coverage` and `StatusFor()` to each building in the city state and notify the hub

## Files involved

- `cmd/agentic-city/main.go` — wire up MetricsWatcher, add CLI flags
- `internal/city/builder.go` — add helper to apply MetricsSource to buildings
- `internal/repo/metrics.go` — already complete, no changes expected
